purpose: Prove IAMScope blocks a boundary-constrained admin path instead of emitting a false validated admin claim.
claim_surface: IAMScope can detect a blocked assume-role chain caused by a permission boundary.
IAMScope can avoid a false positive validated admin_reachability claim on that same benchmark path.
benchmark_proves: For the Env05 alice->admin path, IAMScope emitted at least one blocked assume_role_chain finding.
For that path, IAMScope emitted at least one blocked admin_reachability finding.
For that path, IAMScope emitted zero validated assume_role_chain and zero validated admin_reachability findings.
benchmark_does_not_prove: It does not prove broader permission-boundary coverage across all multi-hop chains.
It does not prove cross-account or SCP-mediated blocked admin paths.
unknowns_remaining: How boundary handling behaves across longer chains or richer policy shapes remains unproven here.
Reasoner interactions outside the Env05 path family remain unknown here.

ground_truth.reason: The permission boundary on env05-devops excludes sts:AssumeRole from the effective permission set.

directly_proven_if_pass: This exact boundary-blocked admin path is not overclaimed as validated.
The blocked chain and blocked admin reachability are both observed for the benchmark path.
strongly_supported_if_pass: The current boundary resolution path is coherent for this narrow real-AWS case.
only_implied_even_if_pass: Broader positive-vs-blocked admin reachability behavior outside this path family is only implied.
